{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment involves the chemical etching of stainless steel plates using a solution of OTS (octadecyltrichlorosilane) and saline. The goal is to study the surface properties of the treated plates, particularly focusing on their adhesion force compared to untreated surfaces.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A person in a lab coat is shown handling a stainless steel plate, preparing it for etching by placing it into a solution of OTS and saline.",
    "The plate is then placed into an etching chamber where it is exposed to a chemical solution for a specified duration.",
    "After etching, the plate is rinsed with deionized water to remove excess chemicals.",
    "The process is repeated multiple times to ensure uniform treatment across all plates.",
    "The adhesion force of the treated plates is measured using a force sensor."
  ],

    "OTS solution",

    "Deionized water",
    "Force sensor"
  ],

    "Force sensor",

    "Lab bench with various chemicals and equipment"
  ],
